page: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DLDSv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, featuring 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 154 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1894.08 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1654.0. However, multi-core performance is weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3869.75 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3522.0. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with Passmark memory scores and cached read-only memory bandwidth falling into the weak and poor categories. Despite these constraints, the server achieves average performance in single-threaded gzip compression, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and static web serving throughput at 6.51 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4lds_v5 offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads that require dedicated compute resources but do not demand large memory footprints. The inclusion of 154 GB of local storage provides a resource-dense option for temporary data handling. This server is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, basic text processing, and small-scale development environments where memory constraints do not hinder execution. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded compilation, or complex multi-core physics simulations.
description: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLDSv5 family, featuring an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 2.8 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 8.0 GB of memory, representing a low 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 154 GB of local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V with dedicated CPU allocation, its performance profile shows average single-core capabilities but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and static web serving perform at average levels, while multi-threaded operations and database tasks are constrained by the limited memory capacity. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for lightweight, compute-bound workloads that do not require extensive memory, such as basic web serving, single-threaded processing, and small-scale development tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight dedicated compute workloads.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized virtual machine with local storage, tailored for lightweight single-threaded workloads and basic web serving.

8.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
154 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core CPU and static web serving performance
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks
